verses.text: Observe the month of Abib, and keep the passover unto the LORD: He brought thee out of Egypt by night. Sacrifice the passover in the place the LORD shall choose. Eat no leavened bread; seven days unleavened — the bread of affliction — that thou mayest remember the day thou camest forth out of Egypt all the days of thy life. Sacrifice at even, the season thou camest out. Seventh day a solemn assembly. Guidance: One week, one place, one menu. God anchored memory in flavor — flat bread on the tongue for seven days will not let you forget the night you ran. Worship is a calendar of physical remembering, not just an idea.

Seven weeks shalt thou number from beginning of corn — keep the Feast of Weeks with a freewill offering, as the LORD hath blessed thee. Rejoice before the LORD — thou, thy son, daughter, manservant, maidservant, Levite within gates, stranger, fatherless, widow. Remember thou wast a bondman in Egypt; observe these statutes. Guidance: Five vulnerable categories named at the harvest table — the Levite, stranger, fatherless, widow — alongside the family and household. The harvest feast is not for the harvester alone. God writes the guest list into the menu.

Observe the Feast of Tabernacles seven days, after thou hast gathered in thy corn and thy wine. Rejoice — thou, household, Levite, stranger, fatherless, widow. Seven days keep solemn feast in the place the LORD shall choose: because the LORD shall bless thee in all thine increase. Therefore thou shalt surely rejoice. Guidance: The third great feast — booths to remember the wandering, harvest brought in, the same wide table. 'Surely rejoice' is a command. Joy at God's harvest is not optional; it is the proper liturgy of the full barn.

Three times in a year shall all thy males appear before the LORD thy God in the place He shall choose: Feast of Unleavened Bread, Feast of Weeks, Feast of Tabernacles. None shall appear empty: every man as he is able, according to the blessing of the LORD which He hath given thee. Guidance: Three trips a year to the central altar. Empty hands are not allowed — but the gift scales to your giving. God reads the proportion, not the price tag. Whatever you bring, He blessed you with first.

Three times in a year shall all thy males appear before the LORD thy God in the place He shall choose: Feast of Unleavened Bread, Feast of Weeks, Feast of Tabernacles. None shall appear empty: every man as he is able, according to the blessing of the LORD which He hath given thee. Guidance: Three trips a year to the central altar. Empty hands are not allowed — but the gift scales to your giving. God reads the proportion, not the price tag. Whatever you bring, He blessed you with first.

Judges and officers shalt thou make in all thy gates. They shall judge with just judgment. Not wrest judgment, not respect persons, not take a gift: for a gift blindeth the eyes of the wise, perverteth the words of the righteous. That which is altogether just shalt thou follow — that thou mayest live, and inherit the land. Guidance: Justice, justice. The Hebrew doubles the word — God's emphasis is unmistakable. Bribes are banned even when given to the wise. No favoritism, no shortcuts. The land's tenancy is tied to the courts' integrity. Crooked judges will lose the inheritance for everyone.

Plant not thee a grove of any trees near unto the altar of the LORD; neither set thee up any image, which the LORD thy God hateth. Guidance: No sacred trees by His altar — no Asherah poles, no idol companions. God's altar stands alone. The neighbors decorated their shrines with groves and pillars; Israel's altar would have only itself and the sky. Holiness refuses ornament that competes.
